message:
fix(storage): harden concurrent schema bootstrap with transaction wrapping and race-safe upsert

Schema bootstrap transactionality:
- Wrap the entire bootstrap() function body in BEGIN IMMEDIATE / COMMIT,
  with ROLLBACK on any error. Previously, concurrent bootstrap calls could
  interleave DDL statements, leading to partial schema creation visible to
  other connections.
- Extract bootstrap_inner() to separate transaction management from schema
  logic, keeping the error-handling clean.

Race-safe schema version tracking:
- Switch INSERT INTO schema_version to INSERT OR REPLACE so that concurrent
  bootstrap transactions that both create the table and race to insert the
  version row don't fail with a PK conflict. Every successful bootstrap
  leaves a deterministic marker regardless of execution order.
- Re-read current_version() after INSERT OR REPLACE to confirm the version
  is correct, rather than trusting the local variable.
- Add a second INSERT OR REPLACE after the migration loop to ensure the
  final version is persisted even if migrations added new statements.

Concurrent bootstrap test:
- New concurrent_bootstrap_on_disk_is_race_safe test spawns 6 threads
  that simultaneously bootstrap the same on-disk database file, verifying
  that all threads observe SCHEMA_VERSION after completion. Uses Barrier
  for synchronized start and cleans up .sqlite3/.wal/.shm temp files.

Connection test enhancements:
- Add rollback metric assertions (tx_rollbacks=1, tx_commits=0) to the
  transaction_rollback_on_nested_begin_failure and related tests to verify
  the connection properly tracks transaction outcomes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
